title: Projects
text:
A data science project that uses the NASA EONET API to track and analyze natural events over time.




R
httr
jsonlite
dplyr
ggplot2




This project retrieves event data from the NASA EONET API, cleans and transforms the dataset, and explores active natural phenomena such as wildfires, storms, and volcanic activity.



The analysis includes time-series plots, event counts by category, and geographic summaries to highlight trends in natural event frequency and impact.







A project focused on collecting structured data from web pages using rvest and parsing HTML elements for analysis.




R
rvest
xml2
tidyverse




This project scrapes data from web sources, extracts tables and page content, and organizes the results into clean data frames for downstream analysis.



The workflow includes summary charts and dashboards that visualize scraped data patterns, such as frequency counts, category distributions, and extracted metric trends.

title: Jayden Lieu
text:
Hi, I’m Jayden — a data science student at the University of British Columbia focused on turning complex data into actionable insights.
I build reproducible analytics workflows, clean and visualize data with R, and communicate results through polished dashboards and reports.

title: Jayden Lieu
text:
What I do

Explore real-world datasets and extract meaningful trends
Build reproducible R pipelines for data cleaning and modeling
Create clear visual stories for stakeholders and technical audiences

title: Jayden Lieu
text:
Featured work

NASA EONET natural event tracking and analysis with R
Web scraping and HTML parsing workflows using rvest
Data visualization that highlights trends, anomalies, and impact
